sources_exist.note: All 7 canonical URL occurrences resolve to accessible pages, although the Slator M&A detail is paywalled behind a landing page. Resolution did not establish full claim support.

figures_match_sources.note: Slator supports 29% LLM use among the 70 respondents already using MT, up from 11%, and 16% building an MT model; it does not support 75% customer inquiries or 45% material adoption across US LSPs. Artlangs supports $0.05-$0.15 MTPE rates and 50-60% of standard freelancer fees, but not the industry revenue split. The cited M&A pages do not expose either claimed EBITDA multiple, Nimdzi has no owner-age figure, and the CAT adoption timeline is absent.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The rebuilt role fractions are bounded and the statutory moat is directionally plausible; current 45 CFR 92.201 does require qualified interpreters and qualified-human review of machine translation for specified critical or technical text. But current_adoption_pct=0.45 is an unsupported size-weighted extrapolation from an MT-using subset, and t50_years=2 conflicts with the claimed 45% starting point and 18 percentage-point annual increase, which would imply reaching 50% in well under one year if comparable. pi inputs also rely on unsupported 50-60% translation and 40-50% interpreting revenue shares.

rubric_consistent.note: active_consolidators includes founder-owned TransPerfect even though the metric requires PE-backed add-on platforms, and the cited page establishes only some 2024 acquirers rather than all five qualifying platforms in the last 24 months. buy_mult and exit_mult lack comparable transaction support and owner-comp normalization. The ESTIMATE_HEAVY flag is therefore unresolved; within-tolerance arithmetic deltas on B and S do not cure the inputs.

cross_checks_honest.note: The no-proxy distinction and statutory terminal mechanism are candid, but uplift treats an 18-point change within the MT-using subset as validation of the all-firm adoption/t50 estimate. Pricing says 75% of LSCs face AI-pricing pressure when the cited Slator page does not report 75%, and terminal value depends on an unsupported mandate-protected segment share.

source_audits.note: The article documents 2024 acquisitions by TransPerfect and Propio and EasyTranslate's acquisition of World Translation. It does not document Big Language Solutions, Cloudbreak/EquitiHealth, any 2025 activity, or PE sponsorship for every counted platform. TransPerfect is described by the record itself as founder-owned and cannot satisfy the PE-backed definition merely by acting 'PE-like.'

The accessible page is a sales landing page saying the paid report includes multiple insights; it does not expose Stoquart/Version internationale, an approximately 5x adjusted EBITDA figure, a 4-6x professional-services range, or a median for $1-10M EBITDA LSP targets.

The article reports that 29% of 70 companies already using MT used LLMs to produce MT, up from 11%, and that 16% of survey respondents were building or had built an MT model. It does not report 75% customer inquiries, large-LSP default MTPE, or the run's extrapolated 45% material adoption across all US LSP firms.

The article lists 2024 language-industry transactions and discusses AI-related valuation pressure, but contains no EBITDA multiples, healthcare-BPO 8-12x range, $5-7B TransPerfect value, implied 8-10x multiple, or evidence for an 8.5x blended platform exit.

The article mentions CAT tools only as a comparison to LLM capabilities. It does not state that Trados commercialized in 1995, that CAT tools reached majority adoption in 2005-2010, or that AI diffusion is 5-10 times faster. The 18-point subset change does not establish a historical analog.

Nimdzi discusses founder-owned small and midsize LSPs seeking exits and a generational leadership shift, but provides no owner-age distribution, no 37% baby-boomer statistic, and no evidence that 30% of US LSP owners are 60+. It also does not document a language-services succession shortage.

The article supports $0.05-$0.15 per-word MTPE versus $0.15-$0.30 human translation, and says freelancers often peg MTPE at 50-60% of standard fees. It does not establish that written translation is 50-60% of 541930 revenue, interpreting is 40-50%, OPI/VRI pricing mechanics, contract-renewal rate compression, or the quoted 75% ALC customer-inquiry claim.

reasons: At inputs.current_adoption_pct.url (https://slator.com/translation-companies-accelerating-adoption-large-language-models-alc-survey/), do not extrapolate 29% of 70 MT-using companies to 45% of all US LSP firms without a documented sampling and size-weighting chain; provide population-comparable evidence and re-derive inputs.t50_years consistently.

At inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://slator.com/translation-companies-accelerating-adoption-large-language-models-alc-survey/), provide findable evidence for CAT-tool time to majority adoption and the claimed AI speed multiplier rather than attaching the analog to a page that reports neither.

At inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://contentech.com/top-mergers-and-acquisitions-in-translation-industry-2024/), name and source every platform's PE sponsor and qualifying add-on within the required last 24 months; exclude founder-owned TransPerfect from a PE-backed count unless the frozen definition is actually met.

At inputs.buy_mult.url (https://slator.com/slator-2024-language-industry-ma-funding-report/), provide accessible evidence for the claimed transaction multiple, size comparability, and market-rate owner-comp normalization; a paywalled report landing page cannot support 5.0x.

At inputs.exit_mult.url (https://multilingual.com/magazine/december-2024/a-dynamic-year-for-mergers-and-acquisitions/), provide a disclosed or credibly derived $10M+ EBITDA LSP platform comp; the article has no EBITDA figures and does not support 8.5x.

At inputs.owners_60plus_pct.url (https://www.nimdzi.com/nimdzi-100-2025/), supply language-services owner-age evidence for 30% aged 60+ rather than relying on founder-ownership commentary and uncited general demographics.

At inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://www.artlangs.com/news-detail/MTPE-Rates-2025--Cost-Effective-Translation-for-High-Volumes), retain only supported rate claims and add evidence for the 50-60%/40-50% segment mix and interpreting contract mechanics before reassessing inputs.pi_dist, inputs.pi_moat, and terminal weighting.
flags_reviewed: ESTIMATE_HEAVY: 3 inputs with quality ESTIMATE (buy_mult, exit_mult, owners_60plus_pct)
ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on B, S
